attacking_style: Attacks on steepest gradients in final 500m-2km of climbs, often after teammates set tempo. Prefers calculated late-climb accelerations rather than long-range moves, though capable of exceptional 80km+ solo efforts when necessary (Giro 2018 Stage 19).
team_tactics: Sky controlled climb bases with multiple domestiques (typically 3-6 riders) before Froome's late acceleration. Team sacrificed individual results to pace back mechanicals/crashes. Dual-leader setups (with Thomas, Landa) created tactical ambiguity exploited opportunistically.
terrain_preference: Excels on sustained power climbs and time trials, particularly second-half acceleration in TTs. Technical descents used as weapon (Mont du Chat 2017). Exposed on ultra-steep 20-30% gradients and punchy false-flats where explosive accelerations occur.
vulnerability: Crashes and mechanicals disproportionately affected him, particularly on technical descents under pressure (Vuelta 2017, Tour 2017). Post-2019 crash showed persistent struggles on climbs, time trials, and even descents. Positioning errors in technical finales cost significant time despite 3km rule.
key_patterns: Attacked final 500m-2km after teammates controlled base; executed on Zoncolan, Cumbre del Sol, Planche
Conservative TT first-half pacing then dominated second half; gained 29s Vuelta 2017 Stage 20
Descent attacks/technical descending as tactical weapon; Mont du Chat, Giro 2018, multiple observations
Post-2019 crash showed complete form collapse: dropped early climbs, 40s down TTs, tail-end positioning
Mechanical/crash vulnerability under pressure revealed nervousness; lost time despite team support 5+ occasions
Team Sky maintained 3-6 domestiques through penultimate climbs before isolation; front positioning prioritized crash-avoidance
form_trajectory: negative: Post-2019 crash rehabilitation showed minimal competitive form through 2020-2021 (dropped early, 26+ minutes lost, unable to work). First top-3 finish by 2022 suggests partial recovery but far from Grand Tour contention level.
